response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software engineer with strong web, backend, and some embedded/C++ experience, but the role is a Principal Performance Architect focused on SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, deep neural network training/inference tuning, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no experience in any of these core areas — no SoC/IP modeling, no GPU/accelerator architecture, no SIMD/MIMD work, and no deep-learning performance analysis. The skill set is almost entirely application-layer software, making this a fundamental domain mismatch despite a long and solid engineering career.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career including some C/C++ and low-latency, high-throughput backend work
Demonstrated performance sensibility (async I/O, caching, fibers handling millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, and open-source leadership
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware co-design, or AI accelerator experience
No deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD background
Background is application/web software, not hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC modeling expertise
